title: Nadiya day 2 strategy
content:
(Maybe things could be added later on because she's quite unpredictable. Or maybe not because I don't like changes in plans. We'll see which one of the two of us takes over.) 
Nadiya will not sleep during the night. She would have spent some hours searching for a place with food (and maybe water) from the moment she left the Bloodbath. She'll try to keep herself as hidden as possible on her search. She should have hopefully found out one by midnight, considering the fact that she is quite fast. When she finds it out, she'll use the clouds around her as bricks to build. She'll make huge cloud walls around the area. She'll also make the walls stretch a bit more than the actual food area. This is not because of any tactical reasons, but because she doesn't like having to stay in one place and would rather move around. She'll leave an emergency exit for herself, but she'll make sure that it is hard to spot, using her camouflage skills. She'll try to put the poisonous clouds on the outer side of the wall, so that if anyone tries to break it with a weapon, they will be sprayed with poison. If she has time, she'll make the place look like it was Gamemaker created, adding details like small towers to make it look like a castle, for example.
  If she has somehow not managed to find a food source, she'll build the clouds castle wherever she is.  
While building this place, Nadiya will be sponsored with empty perfume bottles. I'm not sure how many of them she would be able to afford, but considering that giving her empty perfume bottles is really just recycling, she should be able to afford 4 or 5 of them (please say something about how many perfume bottles she can afford, Corey. This is important). She'll cut off a small part of her sleeping bag using the pocket knife - just as much as it is needed for her to use the cloth to cover her mouth and nose.  
She'll walk fast for about 10 minutes from her castle from the opposite side of her emergency exit about half an hour before sunrise. Then, Nadiya will put poison from the poisonous clouds into the perfume bottles. After that she'll use one of her matches to start a fire that she will put all of her perfume bottles on one by one. The point is to make the liquid poison into gas. As this will take some time, Nadiya will go back to her castle and take the other perfume bottles with her while waiting for the poison turn into liquid. 
 She would have created a human-like figure from the clouds there in an attempt to lure other tributes in. The Careers will probably be hunting (and looking for the person who burnt down the Cornucopia), so a fire with a human figure close to it would catch their attention. Meanwhile the separated alliances would be looking for each other and a fire would signal to them that an ally could be there. Ideally, the Careers and the normal alliances will encounter each other, get into a fight, and maybe even kill each other. Two normal alliances could also attack each other by the fire.  
However, even if only one group goes to the flame, Nadiya counts on them to examine the perfume bottle (which would be at least partially filled with gas at this point, therefore making it look more harmless than it actually is). The poison would be gas, so inhaling it would be the equivalent of ingesting it. Therefore the moment someone sprays the perfume bottle (or even presses slightly its top) they or their allies will be poisoned. If they decide to destroy the poison gas bottle - even better. The gas would surround the entire area for days, therefore making it a death zone, and the entire alliance will definitely be poisoned and die either immediately (due to a lot of poison in the air) or soon after. If someone else enters the area later for whatever reasons, they'll also fall victim to the poison gas. 
 When the area is clear and the liquid poison is now gas, Nadiya will return and change the bottles. Keep in mind that she'll keep a close watch on what is happening (while keeping herself hidden and at a reasonable distance, obviously), so she will know if anyone has created a death zone. If there is a death zone, she'll just light another fire, still on 10 minutes fast walk from the castle, and repeat the process.  
She won't return for the last poison bottle. Instead, she'll let it explode under the pressure of the flame, ensuring that it creates a death zone to defend her castle and that there is broken glass on the ground to also serve as physical protection.  
She'll keep her remaining poison gas bottles with herself. She'll mostly use them as bombs, rather than as perfume, and she'll throw one at every alliance that somehow manages to go near her castle. This would also have the death zone effects, most if not all of the tributes will get caught in the poison and die, and the broken glass will also give them physical cuts. The only alliance Nadiya will not throw a poison perfume bottle at will be Cyrus' alliance if Cyrus is alive. She'll use the spray function of the perfume bottles as a last resort in close fights (therefore if her hand-to-hand combat somehow hasn't worked despite all her experience using it even against armed people). After spraying the poison, she'll run away from its victim. 
When she leaves the last poison bottle to explode, Nadiya will eat something from her food source, put as much food as possible in her backpack, and go to sleep in her castle and not wake up until a cannon sounds or the Anthem is announced.

title: maysilee wins fanfic - chapter four
content:
I feel like I’m posting too much 😭 
SORRY IF IM BREAKING RULES PLS TELL ME ID I AM
Okkkk this part is gonna be about her watching the propaganda version of her Games
(also i realized that in the books drusilla doesn’t break her hip until the victory tour...I’ll just say she was furious at Maysilee getting so far that she walked too fast and fell :3)
”You look just fabulous!” announces Effie. It’s true, I do. My face is a painting of blushes, mascara, lipstick and concealer, my skin smooth and shiny. My hair is left straight-curly and messy, with a black fedora on my head tipped slightly to my right. I’m also wearing sunglasses and a fuzzy black scarf which compliments my hat well. My clothes aren’t all black, though, since I’m put into a dark purple and grey striped bodycon dress. I’m put into perfect sparkly red high heels that elevate me up to 6 feet. 
I’m thinking it’s a little cold though so Effie puts a long black trench coat on me. “Perfect” I say. 
Effie gets teary and smiles before kissing me on the cheek, wishing me luck. I’m put onto a plate similarly to the one that rose me into the arena with my prep team and Effie and I wait for my cue. The anthem blasts through my ears and I can’t even understand what the lyrics are. Caesar Flickerman welcomes the audience and to be honest, I’m not listening to anything he says. I couldn’t care less.
I see Effie chattering with Proserpina and I can’t help but think of me and Merrilee. Proserpina and Vitus’s plates start going up and Effie sobs happily for her younger sister. Effie follows not long after and I hear a smattering of applause. Well, it’s my turn, I guess. The plate rises and I can’t hear anything with all the clapping, declarations of love and cheers.
I’m the star of Panem at the moment, and I can’t help feeling disgusted by it. I’m about to yell profanity at the crowd when the television infront of me turns on, showing the Games.
It starts with the footage aired months before the Quell began to announce the twist. I remember watching it with Merrilee and Asterid in the square, whispering something about his lips. It’s pretty much the same footage, but the lighting is edited so Snow looks more shiny. 
After he announces that “every district will be required to send twice as many tributes to the Hunger Games,” they smash cut right to the reapings. I see Silka fight her way to volunteer and Panache pretty much punching people so he can volunteer. They pretty much rush through the reapings, with all the kids going to the slaughter, but District 12 is allowed a bit more time since it’s the victor’s home.
I give Drusilla on the screen a once over that gets everyone laughing and I shrug, rolling my eyes. She calls Louella and she marches up. Then it’s my name. I see Asterid and Merrilee clutching me in the crowd. The Capitol audience starts sniffing, but I remain emotionless and disconnected. Wyatt. Then Haymitch. I’m surprised how clean the footage looks. I mean, there was literally bits of Woodbine’s head on the ground. I guess they covered it up? Lenore Dove, the rebel, is standing there, emotionless as she watches Haymitch go on the stage. 
Ugh, it’s the parade now. District One is making ridiculous poses in their way-too-bright snot green outfits while we’re stuck in a coal miners get up. I make a comment on it since I’ve been so quiet and the crowd is practically dying for a remark. 
“Somehow they look even worse on screen,” I say curtly, which gathers a lot of laughs.
Anyway, the parade scene is shorter than it actually was, probably to cover up Louella’s death. It’s about time for the interviews.
Caesar’s digs on the Careers have been edited out or shortened, probably to make them appear smarter. Meanwhile, most Newcomers are ignored, and the ones who are included are either the ones who don’t die in the bloodbath (Ampert, Wellie) or the ones who stumble and stutter like the teachers at school. I start off District 12 and I get two insults in the final cut. I can see the money man and cat woman covering their faces with glee and embarrassment when I insult them on the screen. Wyatt is completely skipped and Lou Lou literally gets five seconds of screentime and it’s just her snake hissing. Haymitch gets one remark in but I’ve stopped listening.
Oh look, we’re in the arena now. It’s from the POV of a...girl? I realize from the positions of everyone once she rises that it’s Lou Lou’s POV. They must’ve added cameras in that poor girl’s eyes. Does that mean the Capitol was also watching us through her? How many rebellious remarks of mine did they catch?
I’m trying not to think of it when the bloodbath happens. Wyatt gets stabbed by Panache, trying to save Lou Lou and I see myself fighting and then follow Lou Lou to protect her. I also get a pack. But I feel detached from all the action, especially with all the Newcomers dead in the first two minutes. Only two Careers fell in the bloodbath, both from District 5. 
I see two more Careers die and I see a shot of Haymitch wandering around. Lou Lou leaves the Newcomer base to try finding Haymitch and I leave a little later to try finding her. I see myself shooting Loupe and I watch him fall to the ground on the screen, emotionless. The audience cheers when I kill him, which I find disturbing.
I‘m wandering around when I see a bush of poisonous berries. I apply them to my darts and look around. They also show shots of Haymitch fighting squirrels and Lou Lou’s death. Ampert gets eaten alive and a singular tear finally falls down my cheek, though it doesn’t smear my makeup. Who knows what substances were put into that Capitol makeup.
Okkkayyy I’ll end it here since im at 1% 😭

Name: Pandora Torres 
Bloodbath Strategy: Her priorities are to get as much food and water as possible. She (and Chevy) is from a district that hasn't taught her much about survival in nature, so she will need to trust the food supplied already. If her rebels want her to get something, she'll get it. She will not endanger herself, and she is an over-thinker, so she should be able to properly assess what is a dangerous situation and what isn't. Even if she somehow gets surprised and attacked, she's quite fast, so she should be able to escape. She will get a weapon if she gets the chance, but it's not a priority - she can fight with anything sharp enough, so she can easily craft her own weapon later. She will try her best to find Chevy, so that they leave the Cornucopia together. 

Day 1 strategy: Find a safe place for her and Chevy, explore tne arena, make a weapon if she hasn't received one from the Cornucopia, move enough to make sure that she is safe. She might try to create a false lead to get the Careers (or anyone hunting) away from her and Chevy. She will be open to any plans Chevy has. 

Overall game strategy: Stick with Chevy. No ifs and no buts. Pandora might be able to escape, but he won't, and she wants him to win so that her family gets the victory district bonuses. Now, if something happens and there is the slightest chance that the plan will fail, she will aim for the crown herself. In all scenarios, she will not mention to Chevy anything about this plan. 

If Pandora gets in a fight, she will go completely psycho, maybe even hurting the bodies after she has killed the tributes. It's out of her control. However, she will not harm Chevy.

CHAP 6
The suite in the Traning centre is nothing short of utter luxury. 
My jaw almost drops as we enter; every single decoration within this area must cost thousands. From the thick carpets, to the crystal chandeliers, to the tables and chairs and plants, it’s certainly breathtaking.
“These are your rooms,” Azalea informs us, motioning towards two doors aside eachother. “You’ll be staying in these accommodations right up until the games begin!” She beams at us, smoothing the expensive fabric of her dress free of any bumps.
“Dinner will be served shortly; until then, feel free to make yourself at home,”Apollo tells us. I waste no time in heading into my room. 
There’s a king sized bed positioned in the middle of the room, and a vast window sprawls out, covering almost an entire wall. An en suite contains a shower with a confusing amount of buttons; even at home, with all of my parents wealth, we still just have hot and cold.
I walk around the room, scrutinizing most things. The wardrobe contains an unfathomable amount of clothes; yet, I go for simple clothing and take a baggy top and sweatpants out, trying them on. They’re exactly my size.
There’s a short while that I simply spend inspecting the contents of the room, before Azalea is knocking at my door, announcing it’s dinner.
The table is similarly set to that of the one at the train; a spread of food covers it, their pleasant aromas filling the air. I take a bowl of orange soup, a roll, and a spoonful of what appear to be air-fried potato skins.
“So,” Kai begins, his mouth full, “Any advice?”
Azalea seems slightly repulsed by him, but doesn’t utter a word. I glance between Velvet and Apollo, who are still dressed in the same outfits from the train.
“Not much, for now. We’ll see all the other tributes in person at the ceremony, and that’s when you can really size them up,” Apollo responds, digging into a pork chop. Velvet takes a sip from a glass.
“Mm, what Apollo said. However, I’d like to say; in training, don’t be afraid to show off your skills.”
Her comment seems to bring something to Apollo’s attention.
“Oh yes! Any skills we should know of? You can state them right here, or privately, if you don’t want the other to know.”
Me and Kai lock eyes, and I shrug.
“I’m not bothered,” I answer, and Kai nods in agreement.
“We trained in the same sector in the academy, so I know what Calise is good at, and I assume she knows what I’m good at.”
I nod; he’s amazing with an axe. 
“Great!” Velvet smiles broadly.
I look down, realising I haven’t touched my food. Almost tentatively, I dip a spoon into the soup and take a sip. As soon as the liquid touches my tongue, I feel my mouth burst with flavours. It tastes slightly like pumpkin; which I’ve tasted a few times, during an October Festival; but also a bit bitter, which I like. It balances the flavours out nicely.
Kai clears his throat before speaking.
“I’m great with an axe, and I’m strong - like, stronger than most people, I’d say. Those are the main things. I also took a poison identification class, but dropped out because it seemed pointless. I picked up a couple things, though.”
“Big mistake,” Apollo responds, raising an eyebrow. “However, being skilled with an axe is good; the gamemakers regularly put them into the arena.”
I feel most of the table turn to me, and I set my spoon down.
“Uh, I’m good with throwing sickles. I’m getting better at moving targets, and I can mostly hit in the head or chest. I’m mediocre with most weapons, but I’m just generally good at throwing -” I take a pause to breath, before continuing. My pace quickens, like I’m afraid I won’t be able to get the words out in time. “I’ve tried daggers, like specially designed knives for throwing, but sickles work better for me. I took a poison identification class, the same one as Kai, which I passed and-”
“Slow down,” Velvet says, her tone almost reassuring. “It’s good that you passed the poison identification class, that’ll definitely help.”
Azalea, who has been quiet for some time, speaks out before anyone else can. I sip the soup and listen to her.
“Also remember that being likeable helps!” she chirps, her tone cheerful. “It’ll secure you sponsors, and also win favour with the gamemakers.”
She sips a cup of what I think is green tea, surveying us over the rim of the mug.
For the remainder of the meal, Kai, Azalea, Apollo and Velvet all talk about anything but the games, seeming to want to mostly avoid the topic. I try to pay attention, but exhaustion washes over me, and I just sit, slowly sipping the soup, dipping the rolls in and out of it, and eating the potato skins.
After around half an hour, Azalea stands up.
“Well; I’m fatigued. And you two should probably get some rest; tomorrow is going to be a big, big day!”
Kai attempts to protest, but Azalea blanks him out. Instead, she walks towards the door, her heels clicking against the wooden tiles. 
“I thought she was going to bed?” Kai asks, his voice just hushed enough for Azalea to miss it. Velvet stacks a few dishes up as an Avox swoops in and takes them out.
“She’s off to try win you sponsors,” Velvet muses; I turn to her, quizically. Why would anyone be trying to bet money, this early into the games?
Kai seems to have the same thought, because he goes to speak, but is hushed by Apollo.
“Look, you -” he points at Kai, then at me. “And you should get rest. It’s late. I assume we’re all tired, and Azalea is right; tomorrow will be a huge day.”
I stand up, and so does Kai. After we utter our goodbyes, I begin to walk down the corridor - Kai catches up to me.
“What do you think of the other tributes, then?” he asks. I turn my head slightly as we slow our pace.
“I don’t know. Not all of them look very strong,” I respond, choosing my words carefully. Kai scoffs.
“All of them look weak, I think you mean.” 
He sounds ridiculously arrogant, but I try to remind myself that he’s just brainwashed.
“Whatever,” I mutter. Kai shoots me a confused look, and I understand why; I’m being strangely sympathetic to the other tributes. I take a deep breath.
“I mean, some of them look like they can fight, but you’re mostly right,” I add, and Kai nods. 
“Anyway… see you tomorrow.”
We stand in front of the two doors, and I nod, walking into the room.
“See you tomorrow.”
CHAP 7
When I awaken, it’s still dark outside.
I curl up, heat radiating off my body; I’ve been under these thick satin sheets for the whole night, so it’s truly no surprise.
Nuzzling my head into the soft silk of a pillow, I listen as I breathe; in, out. In, out.
Looking down, the sight of my bracelet - Cara’s bracelet - rounds my thoughts back to District 1. After the Reaping, there’s usually street parties for the families of the tributes. I assume my mother and father have celebrated and celebrated. Tonight, they’ll be watching the Chariots as we make our entrance. 
I feel a twang of sadness tugging at my stomach. My parents genuinely don’t care, it’s evident; they couldn’t even spare me a glimpse of sadness when we were in the Justice Building, not even a hint that they cared that I could be severely harmed. Or die. The only thing they seemed to be concerned about in the slightest is how well I perform in the Games.
I mean, I slightly understand why; in District 1, how a tribute performs is seen as a reflection of their upbringing, and overall family. So if I die, or place badly, it’ll be humiliating my parents.
I sigh deeply, holding my stomach with one hand. God, the past few hours have been such an insane whirlwind and haze of things that it feels strange that, this time yesterday, I would’ve been getting ready to leave my house.
The thought causes me to flit my eyes around; what is the time, anyway? I’m fairly sure Azalea would drag me out of bed if it was too late. I’m pretty sure we’re allowed to leave our rooms before anyone else is awake - it’d be stupid if we weren’t.
I slide out of bed, shuddering as the cool air attacks my skin. Padding over to the wardrobe, I swing it open, grabbing the first hoodie I can find and slipping it on. It’s comfortably oversized, and - thank god - the inside is wooly. 
Quietly, I creep over to the door and open it soundlessly. As I step out into the hallway, entering the living room, I’m surprised to see Kai, lounging on the sofa, a bowl of popcorn hugged to his frame with an arm. Colours flash on his face, hinting that the TV is on.
He looks up at me as I walk further into the room, catching a glimpse of what’s on the TV. 
“Oh, hey.” he greets me, hit attention flitting back to the screen. Moving forwards, I stand aside where he’s sat. I almost open my mouth to speak, when I catch sight of what he’s watching. It’s the Games.
A previous one, obviously. It must be near the finale, because they only cut between 4 tributes; a girl from 1 - who I feel like I recognise -, a boy from 11, boy from 5, and boy from 2. I sit down next to him.
“It’s the 54th,” he informs me, and my eyes widen. Ah, this is Velvet’s games. No wonder I recognise her.
We’re sat in silence as I watch the game play out; they’re in a vast tundra, jagged mountains in the north; a sea-like, frozen lake to the west. The camera often cuts to Velvet, who’s taken shelter in a cave. She looks considerably different; skinner, her lips practically blue, dark circles under her eyes. Kai’s turned the volume down, but I can just make out Caesar Flickerman’s commentary; talking about the odds, how all of them have “fought so hard to be here”.
It slightly nauseates me, so I stand up - which Kai notices.
“Where are you going?” he asks, looking away from the screen. I pause.
“Dining room,” I respond; Kai only nods, but as I begin to walk away I hear him pause the TV and get up, walking swiftly to catch up to my pace.
“Opening Ceremony tonight,” he remarks; I nod.
“Yep. Hopefully we get a good stylist.”
Kai shrugs. It’s pretty difficult to get a bad stylist in District 1, to be honest, because the theme is pretty simple. The outfits are meant to reflect the district and what it produces; and luxury is an easily flattering one. It’s always sparkles and silver - but at the same time, the outfits are always a Capitol favourite. 
As we enter the dining room, my eyes widen at the spread of food. Eggs, bacon, toast, turkey, cereal, avocado, bagels and even more foods I can’t name are piled high. An avox stands by, but aside from them, we’re the only ones here.
Kai and I look at eachother. 
“You hungry?” he chuckles, grabbing a plate and piling it high. I follow suit, and after a few minutes we’re sat at the table, gorging ourselves on the spread of food before us.
After our first plate, we reach for another - when the all-too familiar heels of Azalea click down the hallway, and she appears in the room.
Today, she looks like she’s bursting out of the ocean. Wave-like ruffles protrude from her dress, all different shades of blue, sparkles scattered throughout. It’s so ridiculous, but somehow, she pulls it off.
“Quick, quick! You’ve about 15 minutes before we’re to hand you over to your stylists,” she ushers us out of our seats, and then hurries us down to our rooms.
“Slip some actual clothes on, some shoes you can easily take off, and run a brush through your hair.” The last comment, I assume, is directed towards me.
When I enter my room, theres a sense of urgency hanging in the air. My eyes look around; the clothes I wore yesterday will do. I spot my Reaping dress crumpled in the corner of the room, discarded, along with the bow, but I pay it little attention. Changing quickly, I slip on some athletic shoes from the bottom of the wardrobe, and exit.
Azalea seems to be fully against being even a second late, and she herds us into a glass elevator. Hitting the button, we plunge down. It’s exhilarating to say the least; but since we’re at the bottom of the Training Centre, it takes only a few moments for us to arrive at our destination.
“This is the Remake Centre,” Azalea chirps, craning her neck to look down an expanse of white hallway. Above two doors is the number 1.
I’m led into one, and Kai the other.
As soon as the door closes, I’m left in solitude in the clean white room. Several full shelves line the walls, accompanied by a metal ‘bed’ and showerhead positioned above. To my left is a door, leading to somewhere I don’t know. I’m about to step forwards to investigate when a trio of strange-looking Capitolettes bustle out.
One is a man, covered in iridescent sea-foam coloured scales. Another is a short woman with gems embedded into her skin - much like Azalea - dressed in a ruffled skirt and blouse. The third is another man, who appears to have jaundice by the yellowness of his skin. They all waste no time introducing themselves.
“Hello, Calise!” the first one chirps. “I’m Nix, this is Cona and he is-”
The third one interrupts Nix.
“I am Cosmas,” he says. The second the words leave his mouth, they all pounce on me like a pack of starving tigers.
Nix and Cona take on the task of stripping me of my clothes, and, along with that, the hair on my body, as Cosmas gathers several items and orders them onto a counter.
Gripping the sides of the metal bed, Nix yanks the wax from my leg, grimacing as he does so.
“At least you aren’t as hairy as some of the other tributes we’ve had,” he says, before motioning for me to sit up. “Good news is, we can move on!”
Subsequently, they begin to coat me in a thin, gritty substance that is supposed to remove ‘dead skin cells’ but instead seems to remove several layers of skin. I’m coated in several substances, their smells ranging from tolerable to pungent, before being handed a small cup and informed to drink it. 
They don’t talk much, aside from the occasional muttering - instead, they seem to have fully thrown themselves into the task of making me look wonderful. My hair is washed thoroughly, my body is coated in a vanilla scented lotion, and I’m left smelling like dessert.
Next, they move onto my face, applying a thick layer of foundation after several cosmetics. As they do, Cona comments on how my “features are very harmonious, but my eyes are quite basic”. After they’ve completely refreshed my face, muttering about something called ‘Beauty Base Zero’, they allow me to sit up.
Slowly, they circle me, surveying my body, tweezers in hand. They look somewhat like a trio of multicoloured birds.
“We’ll send Valentina in,” one of them squeaks after a few short moments, before rushing out.
I’m graced almost a moment of solitude, before my stylist walks in.
My first thought is that she’s actually quite attractive. Her hair is a dark red, curled slightly at the ends. Her face is extremely pale, and the only modification she seems to have are two, small red hearts implanted where her cheekbones are; and honestly, they look nice. Her expression is warm.
“Hello, Calise,” she greets me, sitting aside me on the metal bed.
“Hello,” I respond. Valentina surveys me carefully.
“Do you mind?” she looks at me quizzically, mid reaching out to brush a lock of hair back from my shoulder. I shake my head, and she goes forwards.
“I’ve been thinking, and I don’t particularly want to make you look typical, hm?”
I squint. What?
“Typical?” I reply, because I’m fairly sure she is talking about the norm of District 1 outfits; spray painted silver, extravagant headpieces. But they always win favour with the crowd anyway.
“Every year, it's the same old outfits; gold, silver. Displaying District 1 tributes as pretty little dolls, not fighters. I want to shock the crowd.”
I sort of see what she means. But what else could we possibly wear, unless we show up naked. That’d probably shock them.
“District 1 makes diamonds, correct?”
I think. Yes, yes we do, for jewelry and such. But why would Valentina need to know this?
“Yes, we do,” I respond. Valentina smiles.
“Diamonds are very pretty,” she muses gently. 
Valentina clears her throat, tilting her head to look directly at me.
“They’re also the hardest substance on this earth.”

title: Miner's June/July Tournament || Day Four
content:
If anyone receives sponsor gifts they didn't pay for, it's for the plot and no money is deducted!
Three days, and half of the arena was dead.
Royal could hardly believe it. How could it be only three days ago that twelve kids were alive and breathing? And who knew how many days until eleven more would die? Had his father felt like this? In his Games, half of the arena had died by the afternoon Day Two. Lux Hartfield had been responsible for four of those deaths, and would have two more under his belt before the Games ended.
Royal sighed and rolled onto his side. He was grateful for the blanket and food that had arrived in his sponsor gift, but the thought of the dead kids left him awake. Both from 5, 6, 7, and 11. The boys from 8, 9, and 12. The girl from 10. He hadn’t known any of them and certainly hadn’t killed any of them, but he had seen them all alive, just three days ago.
A silver package floating down toward him caught his eye, and he blinked in confusion. Another sponsor gift so soon?
This one was much smaller, and when Royal opened the hatch, he found only a small piece of paper with printed letters on it. He held it to the faint moonlight and attempted to puzzle it out. The… what? He skipped the second word and moved on. Kow? No, know. The X know w-where you…
Suddenly, the second word clicked, and he reeled in shock and fear.
THE CAREERS KNOW WHERE YOU ARE. – M
Royal immediately jumped up and started stuffing his things into his bag. He peered over the side — no loss if they saw him now — and relaxed, just slightly. They appeared to have not acted on their knowledge yet. He could make out two figures sitting in the mouth of the Cornucopia, unmoving.
He had everything in the bag now, and quickly began moving down the cliff. The farther he got away from his old campsite, the better.
Merrick was on the final watch before dawn, with Pyrite. He was angry with his fellow Careers for staying at the Cornucopia. They could have killed the person at the top of the cliff and returned home within a few minutes. On top of that, Merrick was insulted that Marcus hadn’t considered giving him the night-vision glasses. What was so special about kooky Pyrite?
Kooky Pyrite, at that moment, was slowly and carefully removing gears from her watch and the one of the girl from 6, and depositing the gears carefully in a leather satchel. Merrick had no clue what Pyrite was planning to do and, frankly, he didn’t care.
Finally, light began to pierce the sky. The air was gray and misty, and Merrick jumped up and ran into the Cornucopia.
“Marcus! Chisa! It’s dawn!” Merrick said anxiously, shaking both sleeping tributes. The Careers from 2 opened their eyes nearly simultaneously and looked ready to fight, but they relaxed when they saw it was just Merrick.
“It’s dawn!” Merrick said again. “We can go get the person on top of the cliff!”
“We should lay some more traps first,” Marcus said, wiggling out of his sleeping bag.
“Oh my God, we don’t have time!” Merrick said angrily. “They could be gone soon! It’s already light out! Plus, the traps I already have are fine!”
“Why are you so anxious to kill this person?” Chisa asked, suspicion tinging her voice as she stuffed a backpack full of supplies.
Because I’m not going to go down as some no-name loser who died of too much togetherness, Merrick snapped internally, but composed himself and tried his best to speak patiently. “One tribute down means one less for us to fight later.”
Pyrite sidled up beside him, carrying a backpack and her satchel. “That is true,” she mused quietly, making Merrick jump. He stepped away from the pale girl and grabbed a backpack himself.
“Let’s take as many supplies as we can,” Marcus said. He easily slung two bulging backpacks over his shoulders. “What we can’t take, we’ll hide around the Cornucopia, not in a big pile. We don’t want another incident like yesterday. We can leave the useless stuff splayed out. If that’s okay with everyone,” he added.
Chisa smiled. “It’s a good plan.”
Pyrite nodded, and so did Merrick, though begrudgingly. He was extremely close to killing Marcus in his sleep.
Python blinked and sat up. He looked over and saw Aster still in the same spot, sleeping next to him. The events of Day Three had worn them both out immensely, and they had fallen to sleep almost as soon as the sun set.
Now, though, Python’s mind returned to traps. He still had his trap kit, and he definitely needed something to protect himself and Aster from the dangers of the arena. He stood up and walked off a bit into the woods before shimmying up a tree.
It was difficult, but there had been sparse trees back in 3, and he did know how to climb. He hooked his knee for balance and tied a rope around the branch. He bent back the branch and set up the trigger before tying the noose at the base of the tree. Now anyone who stepped on that noose would be pulled into the air, feet-first.
He grinned, satisfied, when suddenly he was alerted by a faint pinging. The sponsor gift dropped down by his feet, and he eagerly opened it up. His eyes widened.
Inside of the gift lay a bomb.
The four Careers moved through the woods in a compass formation, under Marcus’s suggestion. Merrick watched the front, Pyrite and Chisa took the sides, and Marcus himself kept an eye out on the back. So far, they had seen neither hide nor hair of any other tributes.
“How much farther?” Chisa whispered.
“A couple of minutes, I think,” Marcus said back.
“Two minutes,” Pyrite said clearly. “Astute guess, Marcus.”
Marcus smiled to himself. He missed the Lost Boys desperately, but this little pack had its own camaraderie. He would be sorry to kill them, but he had to do what he had to do. With luck, they would all meet their ends by other tributes.
“I see someone,” Pyrite suddenly said, her voice unusually sharp. The other three turned, and Marcus could just see a mop of dirty blond hair before Merrick was off. The other three followed suit, sprinting after the boy that Pyrite had seen.
The boy sprinted full speed away from the pack. Marcus withheld his top speed, not wanting the others to see how fast he could be. They might figure out his secret.
Chisa threw her javelin at the boy, but she missed. The boy turned his head and looked back for a moment, but it was enough for Marcus to see that he was Royal. Merrick had obviously seen too, for he let out a scream of triumphant rage and ran harder than ever.
Royal suddenly went crashing down. He had tripped. Merrick, unable to stop propelling himself, tripped right over Royal and fell on top of the boy. Instantly, the two were wrestling. Merrick had his knife out already, but Royal managed to pull his own from his belt.
Marcus saw what Royal was about to do, but couldn’t get there in time.
Royal stabbed Merrick’s arm, causing him to howl, and then stabbed him, right between the eyes. Merrick gasped and went still as the cannon fired. Royal scrambled up, but Chisa’s javelin caught him in the stomach and he fell.
“You’re… monsters…,” Royal choked out, snarling even as blood soaked his peridot sweater. “That’s why… I-I…” He pulled the javelin out, screaming at the pain. “Mom… Christina… King, I…”
Pyrite’s knife lodged into his brain before he could finish, and he collapsed to the ground, dead. The cannon boomed.
Two tributes dead, in under two minutes. Marcus looked at Merrick. It seemed strange that the angry boy could have died, and so suddenly.
“Get what you need,” Marcus said. He walked over to Merrick’s body and closed his eyes. “Then, we need to go back to the Cornucopia.”
Chisa grabbed her javelin and Pyrite her knife. Both were impassive, though Marcus thought he could detect worry and some sorrow in Chisa’s expression.
Marcus took a last look at the blood-soaked scene before walking away, Chisa and Pyrite trailing behind.
Colette and Katharos were shocked at the two cannons. It was, after all, barely morning, and the pool was already down to ten.
Colette smiled slightly and returned to her painting, which she had begun a bit ago. Katharos couldn’t tell what it was yet, but the colors were pretty. It had almost felt like a normal day, like maybe he and Colette were friends and were having a picnic.
However, the two cannons had spoiled the moment. They were only spaced about fifteen seconds apart, and though it meant two more competitors were gone, Katharos found it hard to be cheerful about the thought of deaths.
Just keep smiling, he reminded himself. Think about something positive.
He scooted closer to Colette. “What’re you painting?”
“Oh, just the sea at home,” Colette said with a faint smile. “What they look like at sunset.”
“I’d never seen a sea until just before the Bloodbath,” Katharos said. “Are they all that gray and choppy?”
“Oh, no!” Colette said. “They’re beautiful! They’re usually deep blue, or green. Only on cloudy days are they gray, and every day is a cloudy day in this arena.”
“True,” Katharos said, and shivered. “I’m not used to the cold. It’s always warm in 10.”
“In my part of 4, too,” Colette said grimly. “I suppose it’s the Gamemakers’ way of making us feel more hopeless.”
“Well, the more they try to put us down, the more we have to just keep smiling,” Katharos said, feeling a bit ridiculous for using his personal mantra out loud.
Colette smiled, a real one this time. “Yeah. Just keep smiling.” The two kids laughed, and for a moment, the arena didn’t feel so oppressive.
Hanya had spent a while trying to perfect her plan, and was finally faced with the fact that she was going to have to fight the girl in the lighthouse. She had no way to flush or trick the girl out, and the only way she could see was to sneak in and kill the girl. Hanya growled to herself.
If only I had drones, she thought. Then I could send them in instead.
Hanya decided that she was going to attack tomorrow. There was no real reason for that, only that she had come up with the plan only that morning and was unwilling to immediately jump into it.
Luckily, she had found a grape bush, which was good for her hunger and thirst. Hanya sighed before lying down and staring at the sky. She almost hoped tomorrow would never come.
Hyacinth was cold and cramped from sitting on top of the lighthouse for a few hours now, and she was tired of the sight of trees. She thought she was due a rest break soon, as she was feeling hungry.
To occupy herself, Hyacinth tried to guess who the two people who had died in the early morning were. She was very observant, and had picked up on all of the alliances.
The Careers: both from 1 and 2 and the boy from 4. It was likely that none of them were dead, but not impossible.
The boy from 3 and the girl from 8. Those two seemed like the most likely, except the 10 that the girl had scored. If only I had slipped two pebbles into my shoe, Hyacinth thought grimly.
The girl from 4 and the boy from 10. Both were strong, but they probably couldn’t match up against the Careers.
And then the soloists, like herself: the girls from 3 and 9.
Hyacinth thought it over. Maybe the girl from 4 and boy from 10 had fought the Careers, and both had died. Or maybe the Careers had killed the boy from 3, and the girl from 8 had killed one of them. Perhaps the girl from 3 and the girl from 9 had fought and both had been killed.
Hyacinth frowned. There was no way to know, and she was tired out from thinking about it.
She stood up, stretched, and walked downstairs. It was about time she had a snack.
Saffron kept seeing the shadows of the cult leaders stalking her. Every time she shot at them, they just laughed and slunk away.
At one point Arbor had chased her, horrifically decomposed, screaming blame. Saffron had finally lost her by ducking behind a tree, but the tree seemed to shift into Wade’s face and whisper for her to kill them all.
Saffron Searson was scared, sleep-deprived, and losing her mind.
The three remaining Careers were more subdued than usual after Merrick’s death. None of them had much liked him, but it was a blow to lose an ally like that. It was later afternoon, nearing dusk. Pyrite had received a small bronze mallet which she had gleefully taken to the watches.
Marcus stared out at the forest, and felt Chisa sit next to him. She held two tangerines and a small, fancy salad container. Marcus smiled and took one of the small fruits.
“Tangerine slices and salad,” Chisa said, plopping down slices on the bed of leaves. “Reminds me even more of home.”
“Porridge reminds me of my own home,” Marcus said, thinking of the Lost Boys.
“That’s what they gave the Lost Boys?” Chisa asked curiously, before scooping a handful of salad into her mouth.
“Yep,” Marcus said, laughing a bit. “The meal of the Champions of Panem.” He waited for Chisa to respond, but was suddenly alarmed by the sound of gagging. He looked over, and saw Chisa bent over double. She retched, and the salad spewed out, slick with blood and bile.
“Chisa!” Marcus yelled in alarm. Chisa’s eyes bugged as she clutched her stomach, retching more. Blood sprayed sickeningly out her mouth, creating a puddle in the grass. Some drops landed on Marcus’s pants.
There could only be one thing causing Chisa’s condition. Poison.
He looked at Pyrite, but she was just as alarmed as he was, clutching her bronze mallet to her chest. Helplessly, Marcus took Chisa’s hand as she collapsed. Her eyes met his, and he watched the life fade from the closest person he could call a friend in the arena. No tears gathered, but he felt a painful weight in his chest as the cannon fired.
He effortlessly scooped up Chisa, and walked over to the middle of the plain. He arranged her arms in a dignified manner and closed her eyes. Then he slipped his partially peeled tangerine into his district partner’s hand, and walked away. He didn’t look back as the hovercraft materialized and took Chisa away from the arena. He didn’t want to see that she was gone.
The boy from 1.
The girl from 2.
The boy from 4.
Pyrite and Marcus didn’t need to look, and they didn’t want to see those three faces flash across the sky.
Python and Aster gaped at the sky, unable to think of why three of the most dangerous tributes had died.
Hanya smiled slowly as the anthem faded out. This improved her odds immensely.
Colette felt a pang at Merrick but all in all was relieved at the three deaths. Katharos was just quiet.
Saffron didn’t see the three dead. She saw Aster T., Arbor, and Wade, and pressed herself into a ball, her sanity hanging by a single, fraying thread.
Hyacinth smirked and leaned back against the lighthouse. Only eight more left to kill.
KILLS:
Royal Hartfield: 1 [DECEASED]
Pyrite Soleil: 1
Khia Neriah: 1 [DECEASED]

title: Surprise! Hychel Story.
content:
No Nonsense. I’ll get to the point.
I must thank Sav over on discord for concepting Dennis Fling’s fan-descendant.
Apologies for the poorly written nature of this fictional story, for a fictional story. I have a habit for making shorter works.
Doppelgänger:
Everyone has their exact carbon copy, it is said. Staphylus Fling wasn’t drunk when he was coming face-to-face with the tributes in his care. A pessimist, an optimist, and a sass machine. Nothing special or out of the ordinary. Then, his eyes met with the final name. “Hychel.” An 18-year-old boy reaped by chance… right? Staphylus’ eyes met with Hychel’s and then, it hit him. The eyes, the hair, all of it. He was a relative of a long-forgotten tribute, dead to the world that his Grandfather Dennis told stories of. The boy that died before the games started.
It wasn’t long until the train ride back to the capitol. Anion, Fisser, and Potena were preoccupied with strategizing and socializing. Hychel was on his own, monitoring the reapings via television and observing the natures of every last person. Was this the person Hy would have been if his asthma never took him? He’d never know for certain but Staphylus & Hychel conversed during the ride. When night fell, and Staphylus went to bed, in his dreams he saw the two figures in his head. Hy and Hychel. Doppelgangers, yet always in one person. To him, it was a potential case of rebirth, one worthy of telling his grandfather Dennis about. As soon as the arrival to the capitol came the next morning, Staphylus escorted the 5s to the lines headed towards the tribute center, and as he went off to the academy, his eyes could only focus on the orange that Hychel wore. Even when he was focused on his assignments for the day, his mind couldn’t help but wander to the thought of Hychel. He was a real-life case of the doppelgänger legend.
Their reunification at the highrise was a silent one. Hychel nodded when his eyes met with Staphylus. The two refuse to speak a word to each other, for different reasons. Hychel wanted to maintain a professional but neutral relation with his escort, while Staphylus felt he personally should not talk to districtfolk, no matter how kind. The night went by in a melancholic manner. Staphylus kept watch of the place while Hychel spent his night time watching overviews of the tributes brought on by CapitolTV. As soon as the strategizing came to an end, Hychel was taken to the depths of Sub-A, to await his fate. All Staphylus could do was watch on when he went to the academy, sitting beside Nora Canville and Leonis Anderson. He couldn’t save Fisser and Potena from a bloody fate within the first minutes of the games, but Hychel made it out alive as did the only remaining girl of 5. Days came and went, with escorts leaving day by day. Then, came the eruption. Staphylus’ eyes met with the golden lava that filled the crevasses of the rocks, slowly creeping its way to the tributes who took shelter on the mountain. Hychel’s eyes were face to face with a fate he couldn’t outrun, but he took it with the best final bow he could. Kneeling before his fate and soon was scorched to nothing but ash. With that, orange disappeared from the color wheel of these games for good.
Staphylus felt numb. He lost the games to a decision done by the gamemakers on purpose and it was only one person to blame for him, even if she was gone and dead to the world. As he took his leave for the day, he witnessed Nora’s breakdown. She was gone beyond measure. Her sanity was taken long ago. At this moment, he didn’t feel a thing. All he knew is that the doppelgangers were gone. No more. What happened once couldn’t happen again in his eyes. Hychel would be nothing more than a memory in his mind, and forever the link to who Hy could have been. Honorable. He respected that nature.

If you would like to join my Club- HFSC- Hayffie shipper club- You can MW me or just comment on this post! Our motto is "Don't be a stranger." (Maybe, I just made that up, I am willing to change it.) or maybe.. "HAYFFIE4EVA"??
HFSC MEMBERS:
@SLAYMITCH2 @The crank @VeiledWriter @Coreydorey @TheOfficialWinterTea @Jesperfahey13 
Anyway... PROLOGUE!!!
As me and Katniss descend the stairs, my eyes land on Effie. It looks like she decided to switch back to her usual Capitol look, which surprisingly disappoints me.
“I couldn’t let you go without a proper goodbye.” Effie says, wrapping her arms around Katniss. “It’s your job to take care of yourself now, Katniss.” She pauses.
“Promise me you’ll find it.”
“Find what?” Katniss asks.
“The life of a Victor.” Effie whispers, so quietly I can hardly hear her.
“Goodbye, Effie,” Katniss whispers, “Goodbye my girl.” They hug again, and then Katniss walks past Effie; my eyes landing on Effie as she walks up to me.
I smile a bit and lean into her. “Effie, don’t be a stranger.” And then I kiss her cheek.
I pull back a bit and look into her eyes, full of an unnecessary amount of makeup.
I didn’t lie when I told her I liked her better without it.
I kiss her lips.
It’s a light kiss, but it brings back sad memories. I decide to wave those memories away for now as I start to walk away, but Effie stops me.
“Take care of her.”
I don’t hesitate when answering. “I will.”

title: A poem for Johanna
content:
(This is dedicated to all the people leaving the wiki rather for good or momentarily. In my mind, we’re all just a group of friends in another universe, and friends miss friends when they go, so I miss you all leaving too.) 
When life has me breaking on my knees,
Take me back to where the trees blaze in evergreen.
Where you can tell the season by the plants and leaves.
Where the trees blaze in evergreen.
Where I can count on the floorboards to creak.
Where the thunder and lightning speak.
Where my heartbeat isn’t taxed on decree.
Where the trees blaze in evergreen.
Where my friends grew up aside one another.
Where the birds chase each other.
Where the night’s whispers soften into seas,
Where the trees blaze in evergreen.
Not where we look up to those in ivory-towers,
To those who forget about the blue-collared hours.
Not where we put those that claim important on a pedestal.
Not where insanity is more than hypothetical.
Take me back to where I can trust the sky.
To where the flowers I pick are mine.
Where I don’t have to choke on debris to breathe.
Where the trees blaze in evergreen.

title: Grains of Hope: The 23rd Quarter Quell | Chapter Two: Reaping Day
content:
Chapter two is finally finished! I apologise for the wait, school was consuming a lot of my energy. Hope you enjoy!
Part Two: The Reaping
Song: Team - Lorde
Zea, Southeastern District Nine, July 4th, 06:34
‘We live in cities, you’ll never see on screen’
My head spirals as I make an attempt to dislodge myself from my ‘bed’. As soon as I finally do so I get hit with the aroma of my fate. It’s in the air. As a stride toward the door which had long been of its hinges, I take caution not to wake Kea up, he could use the sleep. I descend to the living room, where I spy my morphling brother and sister sitting in front of the tv.
“Bro who is that…” I hear Brynlea asking disorderly.
“I dunno sis…” I see my brothers gloomy sleep-deprived eyes glare at me, showing they’d forgotten me again, “who are you and why are you her-“ Acklea is cut off by a slightly more energetic Brynlea.
“It’s starting!” she shouts.
Before a shot Capitol propaganda is directly flashed in my face, I sprint over to whatever we call our ‘kitchen’ and start to use what we have left of our tesserae to make Kea a roll, just like Thornlea had done for me previously. Thinking about Thornlea hurts, especially today of all days. Yet again I’m reminded of what happened on this day three years back…
“And for the male tribute, it is…Thornlea Graden!” I remember the stupid escort with puke green her exclaim on that frightful day five years back. “Take care of Kea for me. I’ll be back soon!” We’re the last words I ever heard from him that were unfiltered by the stupid Capitol. I could barely render his death, and Brynlea and Acklea took to using even more morphling than they had before. Now they barely remember me or Kea. Even Kea remembers his uncle better than his brother or sister. Really Thorn was my brother when Ack decided he didn’t want to be.
There seems to be two prophecies our family seems to have. One being dying of an overdose after your final child is born, or die in the games before you are even close to that. You’re doomed Zea. You always have been. Today it’ll be you. I get shook off balance by this, and I slit my palm. Drat. As I try to clean up the maroon metallic liquid, I see a familiar face walking down the staircase which seems to be screaming from its constant use. “Is this for me?” Asks Kea.
“You betcha!” I say, mimicking the positive energy that Thorn had radiated.
“Is your hand ok?” He asks
“Yeah, it was just a little slip up. Nothing to worry about!” I conceal my emotion with a smile.
“Come on the good bit’s starting! You can’t miss that!”
“Fine!”
“On the historic 575th anniversary of these games, as a reminder to tributes that they are in this together, there shall be eight tributes reaped from each district. Though as a show of our generosity, there will be an allowance for tributes to win alongside one another, with the only precedent being that they are district partners.”
Omer, Northwestern District Nine, 07:16
“And everyone’s competing for a love they won’t receive”
I am greeted to a smile on Elara’s face as I cascade down the stairwell. “Pancakes!” She shouts happily. “They look lovely!” I attempt to match her energy, “by any chance would you have listened to what the quell was for this year?” Elara quotes the entire speech word for word. “I also calculated your chances of being reaped! She points to a sheet of paper with calculations. I can tell by the way they look that she had wrote them that she’d used a different ink than what she’d usually write with. “Don’t tell me you went to the city square again. They don’t use blue ink anywhere else, Elara.”
“I… I needed to access the risk somewhere everything would be stored.”
“But you know that we don’t go into the square.”
“I’m sorry. It won’t happen again.” Elara says as she throws herself out of the room. Just like Caleb tried to do…
stare at the sheet in front of me, dotted in splotched of blue supposedly suggesting my fate. She’ll never know… that I have over twenty names in that bowl. That I owed it to Dani. “
“I’ll see you soon,” I shout just loud enough so that the sound lingers on the other side of the ebony door.
Zea, Southeastern District Nine, 09:39
“Dancin’ around the lies we tell”
There’s a new patch of maroon liquid on my hand. I’m shoved in conflicting directions by the faces of other people just as unloved as I am. I end up standing next to this girl I faintly recognise, who I can tell is trying to stop the tears from flooding her pink cheeks. A lot of the twelve year olds rows in front of me look even worse. I anticipate the stupid escort’s clown face smiling eerily back at as she begins the ceremony. I barely make it through the next fifteen minutes. I watch as she shoves her hand in the girls’ bowl, smiling as she sentences a literal child to death.
“zEa gRaDeN!” She smiles. I can feel Kea’s tears from a mile away. Suck it up. I am marched up to the stage, being almost stabbed by more than a few bayonets. My whole body is numb while I try to maintain the little balance I possess. The escort sentences three other girls to death. Two of them seem to be staring at each other as if they know something’s up. One of them I remember as being the girl who performed at one of the events Thornlea had taken me to. The escort smiles, another reminder that I am more doomed than him.
Omer, Northwestern District Nine, 10:01
“Wait till your announced, we’ve not yet lost our good graces”
I break as I see even the oldest kids failing stop crying. I can barely even try to stop myself from doing the same. I see the escort reach her powder-green hand into the second bowl. My breathing practically stops. I can see Dani eyeing me. I can tell they’re saying “It’s gonna be more than fine. It’s just a stupid luck thing you know?” My mind loses track of the names. And I can’t barely see anything. Just as my ears pick up “OmEr BuCkLeY!” I feel my head thrash against the concrete and everything else just slips away...
—————————————————————————

title: am i the seashell guy (gal? girl??) yet?

content:
Interview
Serenity wore a long and flowing red dress with a long slit down the side that ended at her hips. Her white hair was tied in an elaborate style, and makeup made her face look better than it was before. She wore red laced ankle heels that glimmered in the light, and she wore a smile on her face that was a mix between Let's make out and I want to kill you. When her name was called, she strutted onto the stage feeling confident as ever, blowing a kiss to the audience before promptly sitting down across from Persephone.
Persephone clearly was disgusted by her, but quickly hid it with a flashy smile for the audience.
"So, Serenity. How do you feel about this year's Quarter Quell twist?"
"Well, I would actually quite like it if not for me being in it. However, here I am." She winked at the audience. "The peacekeepers thought this twist was a perfect opportunity to get rid of me for good. But as they will quickly discover; I am not leaving that easy. I plan to make their plan backfire. I plan on winning, and going home to shove it in their faces."
Persephone nodded before voicing her next question. "Well, you clearly have some strong opinions on the peacekeepers. Tell me, if you could say anything to the Capital with no consequences, what would it be?"
Serenity smiled and turned to the camera, winking before speaking. "I would tell them they are free to visit me anytime, but to not be surprised if our date turns into a massacre."
Persephone pursed her lips as the audience let out mixes of gasps, sighs, and shouts, and then she cleared her throat. "I think speak for all of us when I say that you are very beautiful. Would you say that your looks could benefit you in the games at all?"
Serenity turns to Persephone, scanning her with a smirk before answering. "Well obviously." She then turned her head to face the door in which she came through, where the rest of the tributes stood waiting. "Don't be afraid, Loves. If any of you are looking for an end-of-life kiss, I'm happy to oblige." She then turned to Persephone with a smile. "Emphasis on end-of-life." 
And just as she spoke her final words, the buzzer sounded and she stood and strutted out off the stage, brushing her hair over her shoulders and blowing one last kiss into the crowd.

(took this as a chance to revamp one of my favorite hg ocs, Alice!)
 
Tribute Form:
Name: Alicene 'Alice' Livis
District: 8
Gender/Pronouns: Demi girl, she/they
Age: 17
Appearance: Alice has long wavy red hair like wildfire, it goes down to her waist and curls at the end. Her eyes are forest green and almond shaped, she is tan skinned and has freckles scattered around her face. 
Personality: 
At a first glance, Alice generally seems harmless. She talkative, polite, and energetic, along with having good charisma and being very sassy. She acts sarcastic and sometimes seems a little psychotic. But Alice hides it to be able to disguise herself better for targets. However, deep down she is very controlling. Alice does not care about others' emotions and considers them too childish for her. She takes advantage of people's weaknesses and uses it to give herself advantages, having little concern how it might effect other people and especially other tributes.

Alice is willing to do whatever it takes to do what she wants, with includes spreading lies and misinformation about people. She is manipulative and wants to make sure that no one could ever harm her or her reputation. Alice tries to "play nice" most times though, so she's not as targeted, and she usually is able to charm others well. Alice is also very strategic and calculating, taking in information of whatever she can find. Alice focuses on the tiniest of details, observing it from every angle she could think of. She never lets a detail go undetected in her mind, sometimes she is faulty in this though and let's her pride get to her. 

Even so, Alice does have some sort of care left inside of her. She wants to feel affection towards others, but is so scarred from her life that she doesn't know how to deal with the pain anymore. Alice has trouble processing her emotions and mistakes a lot of them for her being weak, or it being a hassle that she needs to forget. Alice is very starved of love though, and the only person she loves is Brian. Alice is very protective towards him and does a lot of the things she does for him. And deep down, Alice wants to feel like a good person but she feels that she's strayd so far from that. 

Backstory:
Alice grew up in district 8 where she was born into an orphanage. with only her little brother, Brian. Their parents had been killed by peacekeepers for suspicious activity when they were children, the orphanage took them in and they've been living there for a while. Alice doesn't recall much about her parents, but she convinces herself that they were horrible people to risk their children being sent to an orphanage to be involved in rebel behavior. 

She had to work in factories and sew clothing for little pay, developing her love of fashion and clothing. Alice was like a machine, sewing as quickly as possible, sometimes sneaking clothes to put in her room when things were slow. When Alice was nine, her and Brian were adopted by an old woman in her seventies named Elaine. Alice remembered begging the orphanage to let her stay, but she and Brian were taken the next day. 

Elaine was stubborn and uncaring towards Alice, expecting the two of them to work on clothing from a young age. Alice and her brother were pushed into working even worse conditions than the orphanage. Her hands and fingers hurt whenever she was sewing, blood tinting her lip red, and her conditions only got worse. Brian would be crying whenever they finished work, Alice hugging him and comforting him. Each day, she began to sneak extra food to him and make sure he had more than she could give. Alice tried her best to care for him, but it wasn't enough. 

One night, Elaine got especially violent with Brian and yelled at him for not finishing up his work earlier and making less money. Alice was horrified and filled with anger, pulling Brian aside with her. That night, she hurrified him away from Elaine's house and they ran away together with all of their things.

The first night, Brian cried into Alice's shoulder. Alice assured him that it would be okay, that they could survive out on their own...somehow. Days passed and Alice survived by catching rats and spilled food on the street. It was awful, but it was all they could find. The two of them were now street rats and hid from the peacekeepers in alleyways, huddled in sleeping bags. 

Apparently, Elaine didn't care enough to search for them or report anything saying that "it would be a waste of energy" Instead, she pretended that nothing happened and that they were still under her care. Even if the peacekeepers found out, Elaine suspected they didn't care too much about runaways and she would just lie that they were unwell. 

One day, Alice was lying on the street to look for food. Suddenly a peacekeeper noticed her and chased her around the district. Alice was fearful and attempted to escape, until she was captured by one of them. In a fit of fear, she kicked the peacekeeper and ran away.

The peacekeeper pulled her into a corner, Alice feeling terrified in the moment. To her surprise, the peacekeeper said that he was a spy. He looked for runaways and strays to make sure that no one was harming the peace of the district. The peacekeeper announced that he heard of her sewing capabilities, and offered her an apprenticeship. Alice agreed but quickly realized it was no job  for sewing...The man slowly inched her into realizing that she was making clothing for killers. The peacekeeper explained that it was to quickly get rid of any dangerous people in the district, and that it would be okay. 

Alice was hesitant but knew the money would help her brother, so she agreed.  As she continued, Alice got more and more involved with these killing plans. Once a tailor, now she was sometimes carrying out kills in plain sight. But she had to continue for Brian, who had no idea that this was happening. Sometimes when the kills didn't pay as much, Alice would resort to stealing anything she could find and escaping as soon as possible. When she was nearly caught, the peacekeeper would often cover for her and Alice eventually learned how to get out of sticky situations herself. 

As Alice dawned on her 18th birthday, the reaping was creeping over her as well. So on the day of the reaping, she stood there in silence, Brian in another line. Alice would try to look for Elaine sometimes, but she was sure that Elaine didn't recognize her. 

That's when her name was called by the escort. Alice was in shock as her name was announced, but she stepped up to the reaping stage with pride. Her eyes met Brian's, who stared with distraught eyes. Alice stood strong though. Now had every reason to win for him.

Name: Royal Hartfield
Gender: Male
District: 1
Age: 17
Volunteered
Personality: He had always loved to smile, even as a baby. Born to a previous victor, Lux Hartfield, he could afford to be cocky and laugh all the time. The other thing you could tell about him even when he was a little kid was that he was stronger and faster then any other kid his age in District One.
Backstory: As previously mentioned, his father had won the hunger games, using his pure strength to dominate the 107th games. However, even though Royal was always joking around and cocky, he always felt he had to live up to his dad’s wish. He would sometimes cry when he was alone because of the stress it was being a victors son. Some of his close friends saw through his facade of arrogance and cockiness but everyone else saw a surefire victor. In fact, whenever he was talking to someone, most of them would ask”So when are you going to volunteer?”. He never gave them a clear answer as he struggled with the fact that he didn’t want to fight in the hunger games. That all he wanted was to find a life where he and Christina could be together.
Christina was his secret girlfriend. However, she was also the daughter of a rebel. Her father  had volunteered one year, an unexpected volunteer that caused fistfights. However, when he got on stage, he brought out a flag of Panem and burned it. He was shot on the spot. However, his girlfriend Tapestrie found out she was pregnant with his child. Lux was deeply into capitol propaganda, and unknown to Christina, had been the one to pull the trigger, using his personal firearm. 
However, Royal was forced to volunteer by the academy directors. He tried to appeal to his father. Analeise had gone to the relaxation area to relax, as the name suggested. She knew her father would be telling her her eyebrows were scrunched together and her nose was wrinkled, and to smooth them about before she started to age prematurely; as he always did. And then her brother would laugh and call her a grandma, and she would flick his nose and insult him back; and her worries were forgotten.
However, where she was now was a world away from where they would ever be, and she knew she would never hear her brother call her an old lady again. It had annoyed her when he did it previously, but now she would give anything to hear his voice again. To hear any of their voices again.
Anything except her sister’s life, which she had volunteered for the Games to save. That was the price, though, she thought. Her life, her love, her memories in exchange for her sister’s.
Analeise really needed to stop thinking dark things and focus.
She took a breath, calmed her features, and blinked.
The District 4 boy with dark hair and grey eyes was in front of her.
What the-
He sat down next to her as she stiffened in both surprise and disgust. What on earth was he doing? Had she not made up her mind to kill him not ten minutes ago?
Though, she thought, I suppose he can’t know that.
“I don’t think I managed to get your name,” the boy said. His voice was warm, and surprisingly friendly. Analeise wondered vaguely why she should be surprised by that, and then remembered they were both trapped in a game where everyone was supposed to violently kill each other.
“I’m Connor,” the boy continued. “From District 4.” I already knew that. “And you are…?” Analeise was momentarily lost for words. What in her father’s fresh baked bread was he doing? Trying to form an alliance? With her? The girl from District 11?
When he was so blatantly obviously from the district that had killed her sister?
Analeise realised she had paused too long, and the silence had thickened uncomfortably.
“Ah- Analeise. District 11,” she said, just managing to smooth out the hostile undertone of her voice, and tentatively shook his hand; though she was sure hers was cold and clammy with sweat. She needed to bury her intense dislike of him, she knew. If the Gamemakers figured out she hated District 4 and by extension him, they would use that information. Probably throw them together in the arena for more excitement.
Although, that would mean she would definitely have the opportunity to kill him…
No, she thought. That isn’t fair. Is it? He’s just from their district. But the boy who had killed her sister had won his Games. Analeise had seen him herself, though she had immediately looked away, on the reaping recap. Standing among the other victors, laughing with a glass of wine in his hands. The same hands he had used to throw the spear that had impaled- no. Shut up.
Connor looks just like him. Well no, not exactly… their hair is similar, and skin tone. But their eyes are different.
Why am I noticing his eye colour? Pull yourself together, Analeise.
Analeise coughed. “Um, I’m sorry, what exactly is this about?” She laughed slightly nervously. “If you’re looking for an alliance, I’m probably not really the best material. District Eleven and all, when you’re from… uh, a career.” 
Dammit, why did her voice sound so high? Her words always got so jumbled before they passed her lips… At least no-one could ever possibly guess she was plotting to kill him, not with the flush spreading across her cheeks.
…why was she blushing again?

title: The Rainbow in my Storm - A Hayffie Fanfiction - Chapter 1 - Abernathy
content:

HFSC MEMBERS:
@SLAYMITCH2 @The crank @VeiledWriter @Coreydorey @TheOfficialWinterTea @Jesperfahey13 @AbbyMc2011 @Human person123 @SunnyFox57
Read this quick Prologue before you read this!
https://thehungergames.fandom.com/f/p/4400000000003911438
Chapter 1 - Abernathy
15 years after the war..
“Uncle Hay! Uncle Hay!” Willow squeals every time I bounce her on my lap.
“Willow Primrose Everdeen-Mellark!” I mock, shoving her against my chest. “Your name’s a mouthful, ain’t it?” I tease as she thrashes against my grasp. “Much longer than mine.” I muse. “Haymitch, stop choking my daughter.” Katniss scolds, walking into the room holding a tray of freshly baked loaves of bread. “You came to my house! It’s not my fault you're in here.” I look down at little Willow, still giggling hysterically as she tries to escape. “Plus, she asked for this.” Willow screams in pure joy as she starts to punch her way out. “Dang, child.” I groan, wincing at her punches despite feeling no pain. She quickly squirms her way out and stands triumphantly in front of me, her hands on her hips. “Ha!” She boasts, before whipping her back to me and stomping melodramatically out of the room, snatching a loaf of bread and trying not to wince before she leaves; her 2 dark brown pigtails bouncing against her back as she turns the corner.
Katniss sighs wistfully, handing me a hot loaf of bread. “Like mother, like daughter.”
I roll my eyes and take a bite of the bread. “I don’t suppose–”
Katniss cuts me off, “It’s only been 2 weeks. We talked about this, Haymitch.”
I huff and take another bite of the bread.
Katniss and Peeta practically forced me to take a detox on alcohol. So far I've lasted 2 weeks… or 3 days if you count the times I snuck some behind their backs.
“Eh hem.” I hear a small voice say.
I look down and spot Rye, butt-naked, at my feet.
“Damn, Rye. I thought we talked about this?” I scold the tiny toddler. Katniss stifles a laugh as she looks at Rye. “He just doesn’t listen..” Katniss mutters, setting the tray down and picking up the kid. Rye looks more like Peeta than Katniss, in my opinion. The kid has Peeta’s wavy blonde hair, and the same chubby, bread-stuffed face too. But he also has Katniss’s grey eyes and mischievous look.
“There you are!” Peeta gasps, entering the room in his usual “kiss the baker” apron.
“I’ve been looking everywhere for you!”
Katniss turns and her face immediately warms as she hands Peeta the kid. “I bet you have,” Katniss sighs, glancing at me with a “why is he like this?” look.
“Anyway,” Katniss says, turning fully to me now. “I’m done helping Peeta bake now, if you–”
Katniss gets cut off by a knock at my door.
“Are you expecting anyone?” Peeta asks me; Rye mussing up his hair.
I shake my head. “Not that I know of. Let me check.” I stand and finish the bread before I walk out the door, almost tripping over Willow, who (unsurprisingly) is laying on her back in the middle of the hallway, pointing a bow and arrow at the target I put on my ceiling just for her. “Hey Willow, careful there Sweetheart.” I begin to step over her, but she just squints her eyes in concentration at the ceiling, as if I’m not there. “Move.” She orders me, but she looks so cute that I can’t take her seriously, and I bark out a laugh. “Well now. This is my house, in case you forgot.” Willow looks at my face and narrows her eyes, her expression blank and unmoving; unnervingly like her mother’s. “Fine, fine.” I give in. Moving out of her way just before she lets the arrow fly towards my ceiling. She misses it by a few.. Metres. “Hey, you’ll get there.” I encourage her, smiling at her scrunched up face before I turn and walk in the direction of the front door.
This time the ring of the doorbell echoes through the house.
“I’m coming, I’m coming.” I groan, but before I answer the door, a glass bottle beckons to me from a nearby shelf. One bottle couldn’t hurt.. I tell myself, quickly snatching it and taking a swig before I open the door.
The bottle crashes to the ground, glass flying everywhere.
I’m too in shock to move.
I haven’t seen her in years. She’s been so busy in the Capitol, and I’ve hardly had enough time to check up on her.
But here she is, standing before me with a strained smile, as if she’s been forcing a smile on her face for the past 20 minutes.
She looks more.. Natural. She still wears a wig and makeup, but it’s more toned down than how it looked last I saw her. She wears her usual puffy wig, but instead of a flashy colour– like hot pink or neon orange– it’s blonde. She’s older, obviously, but she looks.. Better.
“Hey, are you Haymitch Abernathy?” I gruff male voice asks. I turn, a lump forming in my throat at the sight of a man about my age, or less. He has short brown hair, turning a bit grey from age, and smile lines along his eyes and around his lips.
“Who’s asking?” I ask, keeping my voice as bland as possible. But it’s Effie who answers.
“Haymitch, meet my boyfriend. Ezra Barlowe. Ezra, this is my.. Old friend. Haymitch.”
I can practically feel my heart pounding against my chest. My mind moves faster than I can comprehend, sending an overwhelming feeling of panic through my body.
When did this happen? Why did this happen? Did our kiss mean nothing? Of course it meant nothing, idiot! It was merely a goodbye gesture. Plus, it was 15 years ago! Then why do I feel like this? Like someone just punched me in the gut and is now squeezing all the oxygen from my lungs? I need alcohol. Whiskey. Liquor. I need to break something. I need to kill someone. Something. I need-
“Haymitch?”
Effie’s voice echoes in my mind, burning a hole in my aching heart.
“Are you okay?”
And then everything goes b      l          a             c               k
